title: GitHub Copilot: Code With AI Now
modules: Setup and First Contact: Getting Copilot Running in Minutes
How to Talk to Copilot: Comments, Context, and Prompting in Code
Your First Real Feature: Building Something That Works
Debugging With Copilot: When It Helps and When It Lies
Going Further: Codex, the CLI, and What's Coming Next
synopsis: You don't need to already be a great coder to use GitHub Copilot — but you do need to know how to work with it so it doesn't send you in circles. This course gets you past the setup confusion and into actually shipping code with AI as your co-pilot.
rationale: Beginner coders are one of the highest-demand audiences for AI coding tools, yet most tutorials assume prior fluency, leaving early learners unable to leverage tools that could dramatically accelerate their growth.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can use GitHub Copilot to write, debug, and extend code on a real beginner project — without getting stuck or blindly trusting output you don't understand.
source_signals: OpenCode – Open source AI coding agent
Code Llama, a state-of-the-art large language model for coding
